scope_note: QA Double Quantum Number for Diadic Fractions cert [316]. Primary sources: Iverson, B. (1975-1996) Quantum Arithmetic vol 1 (QA-1), pp.2-3 (Rhind/diadic definition), pp.53-56 (Double QN algorithm, male/female/double-female families, open investigation statement); Chace, A.B. (1927) Rhind Mathematical Papyrus, MAA Vol.1-2; Gillings, R.J. (1972) MIT Press ISBN 978-0-486-24315-3, Ch.6. QA mapping: a diadic fraction 2/n (numerator=2, per Iverson's definition) is decomposed via a Double Quantum Number -- two BEDA 4-tuples (b1,e1,d1,a1) and (a1,e2,d2,n). General 3-term formula: k=d1*n/(C1-a1) with C1=2*e1*d1; when k is a positive integer: 2/n=1/(e1*k)+1/(e1*n)+1/(d1*n). Male QN (b1 odd, C1=a1+n): k=d1; female transformation: (b,e,d,a)->(2e,b,a,2d); double-female (2*male): C1=a1+2n, k=d1/2. 4-term two-level: 2/n=1/p+(2p-n)/(p*n) where inner (2p-n)/p is a 3-unit-fraction sum. CLAIM: (C1) general formula exact Fraction for all male matches plus double-female 2/71. (C2) 7 Rhind 3-term Cosmos entries match male QN; 2/97 has two valid chains (m=56, m=60). (C3) female transform produces valid BEDA; double-female (2,8,10,18) gives k=5 and 2/71=1/40+1/568+1/710 (Rhind). (C4) all 8 four-term Rhind entries satisfy two-level structure; n=29 BEDA closure: BEDA(4,2,6,8), p=C1=24, inner={e,d,a}={2,6,8}, n=2p-2a-p/a=29; n≠29 inner QN origin open per Iverson. (C5) Theorem NT: scribal notation observer; QN chain, k, Fraction sums discrete QA claims. Builds on cert [315] (Rhind 2/n unit fraction, orbit-family classification).
